epic_name: MVP PRD - Mobile Quarterly Export in 10 Minutes (UK Sole Traders, Bank-Feed First)
summary: Decomposed the epic into 20 implementation-ready tasks across foundations, triage, quarter close, evidence, voice, and validation workstreams, with explicit dependencies and UI viewability requirements where applicable.
warnings: The source epic does not define explicit workstreams or task IDs, so workstream groupings and task IDs were inferred conservatively from the MVP scope and recommended build order.
UI tasks include localhost-based verification requirements per the UI delivery viewability contract; implementation may need a local preview/web shell even if the final product is primarily mobile.
Open Banking provider choice, OCR/extraction tooling, and voice/NLU provider are not specified in the epic, so tasks assume provider-agnostic contracts and swappable adapters.
The epic title in the source file appears with ASCII hyphens rather than em dashes; the output preserves that visible source form.

tasks.title: Define canonical MVP domain model and audit contracts
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish secure, auditable data contracts and ingestion foundations used by all downstream flows.
tasks.purpose: Create the shared data model, audit-change structure, and export-facing contracts for users, bank accounts, transactions, classifications, evidence, quarters, metrics, and rules so every downstream component works from one canonical definition.
tasks.input: Epic data model section, export field lists, blocker rules, and acceptance criteria.
tasks.output: Versioned schema/spec package covering entity definitions, audit fields, enum contracts, relationship map, and export field contracts for Quarterly Pack artifacts.
tasks.action: Author the canonical model spec and validation rules, including nullable behavior, audit requirements, and traceability fields required by ingestion, classification, evidence linking, readiness, and export generation.
verification: Canonical schema covers every MVP entity and all Quarterly Pack fields with no undefined required attributes.
Audit fields are defined for every user-editable classification or evidence-link mutation.
Schema validation fixtures prove split transactions, duplicate resolutions, and evidence links serialize consistently.

tasks.title: Define category taxonomy, blocker rules, and rule-engine enums
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish secure, auditable data contracts and ingestion foundations used by all downstream flows.
tasks.purpose: Codify the MVP category set, business/personal tagging, blocking logic, duplicate-resolution states, and merchant-rule defaults so classification and quarter-close logic use one stable vocabulary.
tasks.input: Epic category set, blocking rules, voice intents, and export requirements.
tasks.output: Canonical enum and policy specification for category codes, tag states, duplicate states, split rules, readiness blockers, and merchant-rule defaults.
tasks.action: Create the category/rule contract, document allowed transitions, and define the ordered blocking queue precedence used by inbox, quarter readiness, export gating, and voice actions.
verification: All listed MVP categories and tags are represented with stable codes and human-readable labels.
Blocking policy marks only the four specified export blockers and treats missing evidence as non-blocking.
Rule defaults are defined for category, business/personal, and split behavior without introducing out-of-scope tax logic.

tasks.title: Implement secure Open Banking connection and account-linking integration
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish secure, auditable data contracts and ingestion foundations used by all downstream flows.
tasks.purpose: Provide the read-only bank connection flow and secure token/account linkage required to import transactions for first-time setup.
tasks.input: Canonical domain model, provider requirements, business profile constraints, and sole-trader-only onboarding scope.
tasks.output: Open Banking integration module with account-linking service, token storage strategy, refresh workflow, and account metadata persistence.
tasks.action: Implement provider adapter(s), token lifecycle handling, error states, and persistence contracts needed to connect accounts and support repeat refreshes without exposing raw credentials in application logs or clients.
verification: A test connection can create and persist a read-only linked bank account record for a sole-trader user.
Credential or token material is stored via secure references and never written to plaintext logs.
Refresh and reconnect flows return recoverable status states for expired consent or revoked access.

tasks.title: Build idempotent transaction ingestion and refresh pipeline
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish secure, auditable data contracts and ingestion foundations used by all downstream flows.
tasks.purpose: Import the last approximately 90 days of bank transactions, normalize them into canonical records, and prevent duplicate records on re-sync.
tasks.input: Bank connection/account-linking outputs, canonical transaction schema, and deduplication constraints.
tasks.output: Ingestion pipeline that fetches provider transactions, normalizes fields, persists bank transactions, and performs idempotent refresh with duplicate-safe upserts.
tasks.action: Implement transaction fetch, normalization, dedupe keys, retry behavior, and refresh orchestration so repeated imports preserve traceability and do not create duplicate transactions.
verification: Initial import loads the target lookback window and persists normalized transactions linked to the correct bank account.
Re-running the same import window does not create duplicate transactions or corrupt prior classifications.
Failure handling leaves ingestion resumable and records partial-sync diagnostics for investigation.

tasks.title: Build first-time setup and bank-connect mobile flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Let users complete setup and resolve only blocking items through a fast, low-friction triage experience.
tasks.purpose: Deliver the user-visible setup path from install/sign-up through sole-trader selection, bank connection, and initial import kickoff.
tasks.input: Bank-linking integration, business-profile constraints, and onboarding journey requirements.
tasks.output: User-facing setup flow with sign-up, sole-trader path, bank-connect handoff, import progress states, and failure recovery messaging.
tasks.action: Implement the setup screens and navigation states required for a user to reach a linked account and trigger initial transaction import without exposing out-of-scope business types.
verification: Access/start script is provided for launching the UI locally and prints the localhost URL for the setup flow preview.
The setup flow is reachable at a documented localhost URL and completes a startup smoke test without immediate crash.
Missing bank consent or provider outage states render recoverable UI instead of blank or fatal screens.
Screenshot evidence of the working setup flow is captured in the verification folder.

tasks.title: Build inbox query API and blocking-item projection
tasks.workstream_goal: Let users complete setup and resolve only blocking items through a fast, low-friction triage experience.
tasks.purpose: Expose the transaction inbox data model the app needs to show unresolved items, current classification state, and blocker reasons.
tasks.input: Ingested transactions, category/rule contracts, and canonical classification schema.
tasks.output: Inbox service/query layer returning transaction cards, blocker flags, suggested classifications, and queue ordering metadata for triage surfaces.
tasks.action: Implement inbox selectors and APIs that project only the fields needed for fast triage, including blocker-state derivation and optional suggestion metadata.
verification: Inbox queries return unresolved transactions with explicit blocker reasons and current user-editable state.
Ordering and filtering can isolate only blocking items without hiding non-blocking transactions.
Representative fixtures confirm the API can support sub-2-second typical inbox loads at MVP scale.

tasks.title: Implement classification action service for category, business-personal, and splits
tasks.workstream_goal: Let users complete setup and resolve only blocking items through a fast, low-friction triage experience.
tasks.purpose: Provide the mutation layer that applies user micro-decisions to transactions and records audit history for export traceability.
tasks.input: Canonical category/rule contracts, transaction records, and audit schema.
tasks.output: Classification service supporting category assignment, business/personal tagging, split percentage updates, and audit-log persistence.
tasks.action: Implement validated write operations for classification updates, including audit capture, conflict handling, and recalculation hooks for blocker state changes.
verification: Each supported classification action updates the transaction state and writes an audit entry with previous and new values.
Split transactions require a valid business percentage when marked split and clear the blocker once complete.
Invalid category codes or out-of-range split values are rejected with actionable validation errors.

tasks.title: Build inbox triage UI with quick actions
tasks.workstream_goal: Let users complete setup and resolve only blocking items through a fast, low-friction triage experience.
tasks.purpose: Deliver the ongoing triage experience where users open the inbox and clear only flagged items using fast taps.
tasks.input: Inbox query API, classification actions, and category/rule vocabulary.
tasks.output: User-facing inbox screen showing flagged items, blocker labels, category suggestions, business/personal toggles, split controls, and duplicate-resolution entry points.
tasks.action: Implement the inbox screen and interaction model for high-speed resolution of blockers, minimizing taps and keeping non-blocking evidence tasks secondary.
verification: Access/start script is provided for launching the inbox UI locally and prints the localhost URL for the screen.
The inbox UI is reachable at a documented localhost URL and passes a startup smoke test using representative fixture data.
A user can resolve category, business/personal, and split blockers from the screen without navigating to unrelated flows.
Screenshot evidence of the inbox triage screen in a working state is captured in the verification folder.

tasks.title: Implement duplicate detection and resolution workflow
tasks.workstream_goal: Let users complete setup and resolve only blocking items through a fast, low-friction triage experience.
tasks.purpose: Handle duplicate-flagged transactions as one of the four export blockers, with explicit dismiss or merge outcomes.
tasks.input: Ingested transactions, blocker policy, and classification mutation infrastructure.
tasks.output: Duplicate detection heuristic/service plus user resolution states and merge-or-dismiss persistence model.
tasks.action: Implement duplicate candidate detection, expose resolution actions, and persist resolved states so quarter readiness and exports no longer treat the transaction as blocking.
verification: Potential duplicates are grouped with deterministic resolution states suitable for inbox and quarter queue use.
Dismissing or merging a duplicate clears the duplicate blocker and preserves auditability of the decision.
Merge behavior maintains transaction traceability and does not orphan related evidence links or classifications.

tasks.title: Implement merchant rules engine and persistence
tasks.workstream_goal: Let users complete setup and resolve only blocking items through a fast, low-friction triage experience.
tasks.purpose: Support simple always-categorize merchant rules so repeat triage work falls over time and blocking items trend down.
tasks.input: Category/rule contracts, merchant strings from transactions, and classification service hooks.
tasks.output: Rule engine and persistence layer that applies merchant-pattern defaults for category, business/personal, and split suggestions on new or refreshed transactions.
tasks.action: Implement rule CRUD/storage and application hooks so new transactions can receive default classifications while preserving user override capability and audit traceability.
verification: Rules can be created from a classified transaction and are re-applied on future matching transactions.
User overrides remain possible and are not silently overwritten by existing rules.
Rule application reduces new blockers without changing transactions that no longer satisfy the merchant pattern.

tasks.title: Implement quarter readiness metrics and ordered blocking queue
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn resolved quarter data into an accountant-friendly Quarterly Pack in under 10 minutes.
tasks.purpose: Calculate quarter completion state, readiness percentage, and the ordered blocking queue that powers the Finish Now experience and export gating.
tasks.input: Quarter transaction set, blocker policy, duplicate resolution state, and classification completeness data.
tasks.output: Quarter metrics service that computes totals, blocking count, readiness percentage, and blocking queue ordered as uncategorised, personal missing, split missing, then duplicates.
tasks.action: Build the readiness engine and quarter selectors so the app can instantly show quarter status and determine when export may be enabled.
verification: A fixture quarter with 200 transactions and 8 blockers returns '8 items left' and the correct readiness percentage.
The blocking queue order matches the epic requirement and excludes evidence-only items.
Export eligibility flips immediately when blocking transaction count reaches zero.

tasks.title: Build Quarter screen and Finish Now queue UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn resolved quarter data into an accountant-friendly Quarterly Pack in under 10 minutes.
tasks.purpose: Deliver the headline quarter-close user journey showing readiness, remaining items, and a focused Finish Now queue containing only blockers.
tasks.input: Quarter readiness metrics, queue ordering, and classification/duplicate resolution actions.
tasks.output: Quarter screen UI with readiness headline, items-left count, Finish Now entry, export gating state, and blocking-only resolution queue.
tasks.action: Implement the quarter-close screens and transitions so users can move from quarter overview to ordered blocking resolution with minimal taps.
verification: Access/start script is provided for launching the quarter-close UI locally and prints the localhost URL for the flow.
The Quarter screen is reachable at a documented localhost URL and passes a startup smoke test with fixture quarter data.
The screen displays the readiness headline and only blocker items in Finish Now, enabling Export only at zero blockers.
Screenshot evidence of the Quarter screen and Finish Now queue is captured in the verification folder.

tasks.title: Generate Quarterly Pack CSV exports
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn resolved quarter data into an accountant-friendly Quarterly Pack in under 10 minutes.
tasks.purpose: Produce the accountant-friendly CSV artifacts with traceable transaction, evidence-index, and summary data for a selected quarter.
tasks.input: Canonical export contracts, quarter metrics, resolved transaction classifications, and optional evidence links.
tasks.output: CSV export generator for Transactions.csv, EvidenceIndex.csv, and QuarterlySummary.csv with deterministic formatting and traceable record mapping.
tasks.action: Implement export builders, field mappers, and file packaging for the three required CSV artifacts, including empty-but-present EvidenceIndex output when no evidence exists.
verification: Export creates all three CSV files with the exact required field sets and stable column ordering.
Transaction and summary totals reconcile for the same quarter and preserve bank/evidence traceability.
EvidenceIndex.csv is exported even when no evidence records exist for the quarter.

tasks.title: Generate Quarterly Pack PDF summary
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn resolved quarter data into an accountant-friendly Quarterly Pack in under 10 minutes.
tasks.purpose: Create the 1-2 page PDF summary covering period totals, readiness, audit counts, and evidence coverage for accountant handoff.
tasks.input: Quarter metrics, CSV export aggregates, classification audit statistics, and evidence coverage counts.
tasks.output: QuarterlyPack.pdf generator with branded summary layout, total sections, category highlights, audit counts, and evidence coverage metrics.
tasks.action: Implement the PDF composition pipeline and data aggregation needed to render a concise, accountant-friendly summary aligned with the CSV pack contents.
verification: PDF output includes period, totals, 100% readiness, category highlights, audit counts, and evidence coverage as required.
PDF totals reconcile with the generated CSV pack for the same quarter.
Generated PDF remains within the intended 1-2 page footprint for standard MVP datasets.

tasks.title: Gate export availability and validate traceable pack generation
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn resolved quarter data into an accountant-friendly Quarterly Pack in under 10 minutes.
tasks.purpose: Enforce that exports are only available when blocker count is zero and verify the complete Quarterly Pack is generated with correct totals and traceability.
tasks.input: Quarter screen/export state, readiness engine, CSV generator, and PDF generator.
tasks.output: Export gate orchestration plus integration tests covering enablement state, pack assembly, and artifact traceability.
tasks.action: Implement the final export trigger, pack assembly, and integration checks so a resolved quarter immediately produces the full Quarterly Pack and blocked quarters cannot export.
verification: Resolving the last blocker enables export immediately without requiring app restart or manual refresh.
Successful export produces all four artifacts and records a manifest or traceable output set for the selected quarter.
Blocked quarters cannot produce a Quarterly Pack and surface a clear reason tied to remaining blockers.

tasks.title: Implement evidence capture storage and extraction pipeline
tasks.workstream_goal: Capture receipts and related evidence without making them export blockers, while preserving confident user-confirmed matching.
tasks.purpose: Persist receipt or document uploads, extract best-effort metadata, and store evidence records for later user-confirmed linking.
tasks.input: Canonical evidence schema, storage requirements, and capture channels from camera/share/email-later workflows.
tasks.output: Evidence ingestion pipeline with file storage, metadata extraction, evidence persistence, and extraction-confidence recording.
tasks.action: Implement upload/storage handling and extraction hooks so evidence can be captured, normalized, and prepared for candidate matching without blocking quarter export.
verification: Captured evidence records persist file location, extracted metadata, and extraction confidence even when extraction is partial.
The system supports receipt/image evidence and leaves unmatched evidence in a safe non-blocking state.
Storage and extraction failures are recoverable and do not prevent the app from loading existing quarter data.

tasks.title: Implement evidence-to-transaction candidate ranking and confirm-first link service
tasks.workstream_goal: Capture receipts and related evidence without making them export blockers, while preserving confident user-confirmed matching.
tasks.purpose: Suggest the top three bank transaction matches for a piece of evidence using amount, date, and merchant similarity while requiring explicit user confirmation.
tasks.input: Evidence metadata, bank transactions, matching heuristics, and evidence-link schema.
tasks.output: Matching service that ranks candidate bank transactions, exposes match-reason chips, and persists only user-confirmed links.
tasks.action: Implement candidate generation, ranking heuristics, top-three selection, and confirmation persistence so evidence links are always user-approved and explainable.
verification: For matched evidence, the service returns at most three ranked candidates with human-readable match reasons.
No evidence link is persisted as confirmed without an explicit user confirmation action.
Selecting 'No match' or deferring leaves evidence available for later review without affecting export readiness.

tasks.title: Build evidence capture and match-confirmation UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Capture receipts and related evidence without making them export blockers, while preserving confident user-confirmed matching.
tasks.purpose: Provide the user-facing flow for capturing or reviewing evidence, seeing top candidate bank matches, and confirming or deferring the link.
tasks.input: Evidence ingestion outputs, match candidate ranking, and inbox/quarter context for related tasks.
tasks.output: Evidence capture and confirmation UI with preview, extracted metadata, top-three candidate bottom sheet, and No match/Later actions.
tasks.action: Implement the evidence flow screens and bottom-sheet confirmation experience so users can attach receipts quickly while keeping confirmation explicit and low-friction.
verification: Access/start script is provided for launching the evidence UI locally and prints the localhost URL for the flow.
The evidence flow is reachable at a documented localhost URL and passes a startup smoke test with fixture evidence and candidate data.
The UI shows up to three candidates with merchant, date, amount, and match-reason chips plus No match/Later actions.
Screenshot evidence of the evidence confirmation flow is captured in the verification folder.

tasks.title: Integrate evidence links into exports without making evidence blocking
tasks.workstream_goal: Capture receipts and related evidence without making them export blockers, while preserving confident user-confirmed matching.
tasks.purpose: Ensure matched evidence appears in exports and coverage metrics while preserving the epic rule that missing or unmatched evidence never blocks export readiness.
tasks.input: Quarter readiness logic, evidence link records, CSV export mapping, and PDF evidence coverage requirements.
tasks.output: Integration updates for non-blocking evidence behavior, matched_evidence_ids export fields, EvidenceIndex linkage, and PDF coverage statistics.
tasks.action: Wire evidence-link outputs into readiness, CSV, and PDF layers so only confirmed matches appear as linked while unresolved evidence remains non-blocking.
verification: Quarter readiness ignores missing or unmatched evidence when computing blockers.
Confirmed evidence links appear in Transactions.csv, EvidenceIndex.csv, and PDF coverage statistics consistently.
Unmatched evidence is still exported in EvidenceIndex.csv with null bank link fields where appropriate.

tasks.title: Implement MVP voice intent parser for micro-decisions
tasks.workstream_goal: Support fast voice-driven micro-decisions that are explicit, reversible, and limited to the MVP command set.
tasks.purpose: Interpret the defined MVP voice commands for category, business/personal, split percentage, evidence attach, candidate selection, and no-match outcomes.
tasks.input: Voice command list, category vocabulary, classification actions, and evidence matching actions.
tasks.output: Voice intent parsing service with slot extraction, normalization, and command-to-action mapping for inbox and Finish Now contexts.
tasks.action: Implement the parser and command mapping for the exact MVP command set, including ambiguity handling and structured outputs consumable by the app.
verification: Supported commands map to structured actions for category, business/personal, split, attach receipt, candidate selection, and no-match flows.
Ambiguous or low-confidence input returns a recoverable prompt/failure state rather than applying the wrong action.
Parser fixtures include examples such as 'Category: Travel' and 'Split 70%' with correct slot extraction.

tasks.title: Apply voice actions in inbox and Finish Now with confirmation chip and undo
tasks.workstream_goal: Support fast voice-driven micro-decisions that are explicit, reversible, and limited to the MVP command set.
tasks.purpose: Connect parsed voice intents to real transaction or evidence actions and surface the required one-tap confirmation chip with undo/edit behavior.
tasks.input: Voice intent outputs, inbox/quarter UI workflows, classification service, and evidence matching confirmations.
tasks.output: Integrated voice-action UX for inbox and Finish Now, including applied-action confirmation chip, undo support, and safe action replay boundaries.
tasks.action: Wire voice commands into the user workflows so voice decisions behave like quick actions, produce clear applied summaries, and can be undone with one tap.
verification: Access/start script is provided for launching the voice-enabled UI locally and prints the localhost URL for the workflow.
The voice-enabled flow is reachable at a documented localhost URL and passes a startup smoke test using fixture or mocked voice input.
A voice command such as 'Category: Travel' applies the intended change and shows a single confirmation chip with one-tap undo.
Screenshot evidence of the confirmation chip in a working UI state is captured in the verification folder.

tasks.title: Add voice success metrics and fallback handling
tasks.workstream_goal: Support fast voice-driven micro-decisions that are explicit, reversible, and limited to the MVP command set.
tasks.purpose: Measure whether voice intents apply successfully without edits and provide fallback flows when voice cannot safely complete an action.
tasks.input: Voice parsing results, applied action events, undo/edit actions, and MVP success metric targets.
tasks.output: Voice telemetry and fallback policy covering command success, edit-after-apply tracking, and safe fallback to tap-based resolution.
tasks.action: Instrument the voice workflow and implement fallback behavior so the product can measure the 80% success target and degrade safely when interpretation is weak.
verification: Telemetry records whether a parsed voice intent was applied and later edited or undone.
Fallback states route the user back to tap-based controls without losing context or corrupting data.
Reporting outputs can compute voice command success rate against the MVP definition.

tasks.title: Implement compliance surfaces and security hardening
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP is secure, performant, measurable, and aligned with the stated acceptance tests.
tasks.purpose: Address the non-functional requirements for token security, encryption posture, and the user-facing disclaimer that the product is not tax advice.
tasks.input: Security requirements, onboarding and export surfaces, and bank integration design.
tasks.output: Security hardening checklist implementation, disclaimer placements, secret-handling controls, and resilience notes for provider-managed credentials.
tasks.action: Implement the non-functional security and compliance requirements across storage, transport, and user-visible legal copy, keeping startup and failure modes resilient.
verification: Required disclaimer text is visible on the relevant setup/export surfaces and does not block core flows.
Token handling, storage, and transport controls satisfy the stated secure-storage and encryption requirements.
Security-related failures degrade to recoverable states and do not expose sensitive values in logs or UI.

tasks.title: Meet inbox and quarter-close performance budgets
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP is secure, performant, measurable, and aligned with the stated acceptance tests.
tasks.purpose: Validate and tune the MVP against the stated performance promises, especially inbox load time and fast quarter-close interactions on typical devices.
tasks.input: Inbox APIs, quarter metrics APIs, UI workflows, and representative transaction fixtures.
tasks.output: Performance benchmark suite, tuned hotspots, and documented SLA results for inbox loading and quarter-close interaction paths.
tasks.action: Create representative performance fixtures and optimize query, caching, or rendering paths until the inbox and quarter-close flows meet the intended responsiveness budget.
verification: Inbox loading meets the less-than-2-second target on representative MVP fixtures and devices or emulation profiles.
Quarter readiness and Finish Now entry remain responsive on a quarter with at least 200 transactions.
Benchmark results are documented with reproducible commands or fixtures for regression tracking.

tasks.title: Instrument MVP funnel and product success metrics
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP is secure, performant, measurable, and aligned with the stated acceptance tests.
tasks.purpose: Capture the metrics needed to evaluate setup completion, export completion, blocking-item volume, quarter-close duration, and voice-command success.
tasks.input: Setup, inbox, quarter-close, export, and voice workflow events plus the MVP metric definitions.
tasks.output: Analytics/event schema, instrumentation hooks, and reporting definitions for median quarter-close time, export completion rate, blockers per quarter, and voice success rate.
tasks.action: Instrument the critical product funnel and define metric calculations so the team can measure whether the MVP promise is being met in production or pilot usage.
verification: Event instrumentation covers the full path from quarter opened to export generated with timestamps suitable for median duration analysis.
Analytics can compute export completion rate and average blocking items per quarter using event and domain data.
Voice success instrumentation aligns with the MVP definition of intent applied without edit.

tasks.title: Build end-to-end acceptance and regression suite for MVP scenarios
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP is secure, performant, measurable, and aligned with the stated acceptance tests.
tasks.purpose: Turn the epic acceptance tests into repeatable automated or semi-automated checks that prove the MVP works end to end across ingestion, blocker resolution, export, evidence confirmation, and voice undo.
tasks.input: Completed core workflows, representative quarter fixtures, and the epic's MVP acceptance criteria.
tasks.output: Regression suite and test fixtures covering the 200-transaction quarter scenario, export artifact validation, evidence confirmation, and voice-action undo behavior.
tasks.action: Implement end-to-end tests and fixtures that exercise the core user journeys and acceptance tests with deterministic expected outputs and failure reporting.
verification: A quarter with 200 transactions and 8 blockers renders the expected remaining-items state and reaches export-ready after those blockers are resolved.
The suite validates generation of all four export artifacts with correct totals and traceability checks.
Evidence matching tests require explicit user confirmation and voice 'Category: Travel' remains undoable with one tap.
